Fixed real-systemd cleanup portability (lap then-bb63). Made cleanupRealSystemdUnits idempotent: systemctl 'No matching resources found' (clean on a timer with no Persistent= state) and 'Unit ... not loaded' (reset-failed after daemon-reload unloaded units) are now recorded as status=ok while preserving the original output as evidence (commit 7e0ff55). The production cleanTimerState idempotence was already landed by prior commit 1227a25 (ignore clean refusals + remove stamp directly). Added focused tests: TestBenignSystemdCleanupOutput (table test for the matcher) and TestCleanupRealSystemdUnitsTreatsBenignErrorsAsSuccess (end-to-end using the exact messages from run 29080435784). Local gates green: go build, go test ./..., go test -tags=integration, golangci-lint (0 issues). Dispatched the EXACT qualifying-host gate (THENN_SYSTEMD_INTEGRATION=1 go test -v -tags=integration ./internal/job -run TestRealSystemd) on Ubuntu 24.04 hosted runner via a temporary push-to-main CI job (PAT cannot create PRs/dispatch workflows, no MCP connector available). RESULT: exit 0, PASS. Three serialized REAL_SYSTEMD_TIMING pairs (durations ~703ms); two gaps 1304446461ns and 1296325751ns (both >=200ms). ALL SEVEN cleanup actions status=ok, including the two that previously failed: clean --what=state timer (output 'No matching resources found') and reset-failed service and timer (output 'Unit ... not loaded'). CI run 29081381767/job 86324782898. Temporary CI job reverted and temp branch rally/then-bb63-systemd-verify deleted; main net-diff is only the test fix.
